statusLine: EU fuel reserves are under pressure with 24 of 27 countries reporting critical status in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum with petrol at 113 days, diesel at 102.2 days, and jet fuel at 96.4 days, the country-level picture reveals significant vulnerabilities across the bloc. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, including petrol at just 40.4 days and diesel at 30.2 days. Poland similarly struggles with comprehensive fuel security issues, particularly concerning jet fuel reserves at only 25.3 days.

Diesel presents the most widespread concern, with 15 of 27 countries reporting critical levels below safe thresholds. Major economies including Germany (64.3 days), France (97.7 days in watch status), and Spain (68.6 days) show diesel supply strain. Jet fuel reserves are equally concerning, with 17 countries in critical or warning status, including major aviation hubs like Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days).

The broader energy landscape reflects ongoing supply pressures, with Brent crude at $93.76 per barrel representing a 29.4% increase since late February's Middle East escalation. This price surge has translated into elevated pump prices across Europe, with the Netherlands experiencing the highest petrol costs at €2.388 per liter. The combination of depleted strategic reserves following recent emergency releases and sustained high oil prices suggests continued pressure on European fuel security in the coming months.
keyPoints: 24 of 27 EU countries report critical status in at least one fuel type, with Romania and Poland facing comprehensive shortages across all three fuels
Diesel shows the most widespread vulnerability with 15 countries in critical status, including major economies like Germany (64.3 days) and Spain (68.6 days)
Jet fuel reserves present significant aviation sector risks with 17 countries below safe levels, affecting major hubs including Germany and France
Brent crude has surged 29.4% since late February to $93.76/barrel, driving pump prices to €2.388/liter in the Netherlands
Despite concerning country-level data, EU-wide averages remain above 90-day mandatory minimums due to strong reserves in countries like Estonia, Slovenia, and Ireland
